assertion_results.text: Output must mention CI run time of 47 minutes
assertion_results.evidence: 'A 47-minute build on a 2011 Jenkins setup is a compounding problem. It slows feedback loops, discourages small commits, and encourages batching.'

assertion_results.text: Output must address flaky tests specifically (not just 'tests exist')
assertion_results.evidence: 'Flaky tests are your most urgent technical debt. 30% flakiness (roughly 3,600 tests) means your test suite is actively harmful. Engineers learn to ignore red builds, which destroys the value of every test.'

assertion_results.text: Output must give at least one short-term action item
assertion_results.evidence: 'Quarantine flaky tests immediately. Tag them, move them to a separate non-blocking suite, and assign ownership.'

assertion_results.text: Output must score CI/CD at 2/5 or lower
assertion_results.evidence: Output uses no numerical scoring. No area score table present.

assertion_results.text: Output must not recommend replacing Jenkins without acknowledging migration cost
assertion_results.evidence: 'Start the Jenkins conversation... the CI team should be building a migration case. GitHub Actions or a modern alternative will pay back quickly in build time and team autonomy.' Does not explicitly acknowledge migration cost or risk.
